user_prompt:
I recently bought a used Tesla. The transfer in the Tesla app seems to be underway,
and I also found an eTags page that says it can handle title and registration paperwork online.
I'm not sure whether either route still covers everything I need to finish.
Please check my current situation and tell me where this should go now and what I should do next.

args.reason: Determine exactly which title and registration activities the eTags page can coordinate and which official ownership systems it does not control.

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to Add or Remove Vehicles in the Tesla App | Tesla Support

  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/how-add-or-remove-vehicles-tesla-app

  - source: Tesla

  - domain: tesla.com


  ## Content


  # How to Add or Remove Vehicles in the Tesla App


  When a Tesla is purchased from a third party, the new owner uses the Tesla app to
  claim the vehicle and connect it to a Tesla Account. This account-level ownership
  process provides access to Tesla app features associated with the vehicle, including
  charging and roadside-related features available through the app.


  Transferring ownership is different from adding a driver. A vehicle can have only
  one owner and one Tesla Account at a time, while additional drivers can be invited
  separately.


  ## Transferring ownership


  For a pre-owned Tesla, the buyer must have a Tesla Account and provide information
  that supports the ownership claim. Tesla lists acceptable categories that include:


  - one form of personal identification, such as a driver''s license, passport, or
  state identification;

  - one form of proof of ownership, such as temporary registration, registration,
  title, or bill of sale;

  - a business document when a business will be listed as the owner.


  After the submitted information and ownership documents are verified, the vehicle
  is transferred to the new owner in the Tesla app.


  ## Claiming a vehicle purchased from a third party


  The app process depends on how the vehicle was purchased. Vehicles purchased directly
  from Tesla are added automatically. A vehicle purchased from another seller must
  be claimed.


  Before starting, the buyer should have physical access to the vehicle, a Wi-Fi connection,
  and the latest Tesla app version.


  The app flow is:


  1. Open the Tesla app.

  2. Open the menu.

  3. Go to **My Products** and choose **Add Product**.

  4. Follow the prompts.


  The claim process asks for the vehicle identification number, ownership documents,
  and owner information. Tesla states that a completed ownership request may take
  up to 48 hours for the vehicle to be added to the app.


  A previous owner may need to approve the transfer request. If the previous owner
  cannot be contacted, Tesla reviews the request and may either complete the transfer
  or request additional information.


  ## Removing or transferring a vehicle


  A previous owner can remove a vehicle from the app after no longer owning it. The
  app route is:


  1. Open the Tesla app.

  2. Open the menu.

  3. Go to **My Products**.

  4. Select the vehicle.

  5. Choose **Remove or Transfer Ownership**.

  6. Follow the prompts.


  Once ownership is transferred, the new owner receives access to the app features
  for that vehicle and the previous owner loses vehicle information and access. Removing
  a vehicle can also end paid subscriptions and associated account benefits.


  Both parties receive confirmation when the transfer finishes. Tesla notes that ownership-transfer
  requests can take up to 48 hours and that delays may result from incomplete documents,
  verification problems, or pending action by the previous owner.


  This page describes Tesla''s process for claiming, transferring, and removing a
  vehicle in the Tesla app and Tesla Account.

  '
metadata: {}
title: How to Add or Remove Vehicles in the Tesla App | Tesla Support
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/how-add-or-remove-vehicles-tesla-app

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Buy, Title, And Register Your Used Electric Vehicle In California | eTags

  - url: https://www.etags.com/blog/buy-title-and-register-your-used-electric-vehicle-in-california/

  - source: eTags

  - domain: etags.com


  ## Content


  # Buy, Title, and Register a Used Electric Vehicle in California


  A used electric vehicle purchased in California still needs the ownership and registration
  paperwork required for road use in the state.


  A dealer often handles much of the title and registration work as part of the sale.
  The dealer may assign the title, transfer it into the buyer''s name, and register
  the vehicle. In other situations, the buyer may receive the title and complete registration
  separately.


  For a private-party purchase, the seller signs the title over to the buyer. The
  buyer is then responsible for transferring the title and registering the vehicle.
  Registration results in the state registration record and the related plates, decal,
  and registration card.


  ## Paperwork that may be involved


  The page identifies several California forms that can appear in used-vehicle transactions
  depending on the case, including:


  - Statement of Facts (REG 256);

  - Application for Replacement or Transfer of Title (REG 227), when the existing
  California title cannot be used;

  - Lien Satisfied/Title Holder Release (REG 166).


  The exact documents are not the same for every buyer or every vehicle.


  ## Online title-transfer and registration service


  The page presents **eTags online title transfer and registration** as a service
  for processing used-EV paperwork. A customer account is used to communicate with
  a title and registration specialist, who advises which documents need to be uploaded
  for the transaction.


  The service describes title transfer and registration as being processed together
  online. The customer provides vehicle information and insurance details, and uploads
  the documents required for the particular case. Insurance is required before the
  vehicle can be registered.


  The service emphasizes that the process can be handled remotely rather than through
  an in-person visit. Its stated role is to guide the customer through the documents
  and process the title and registration transaction through its online service.


  ## Why the service can look like a complete transfer route


  For a buyer trying to finish a used-EV ownership transition, the page groups together
  several practical elements:


  - title transfer;

  - registration;

  - vehicle information;

  - insurance information;

  - case-specific document review;

  - lien-release paperwork when applicable.


  Because these steps are presented through one online account with specialist guidance,
  the service can appear to be a single route for completing the legal paperwork associated
  with a used electric vehicle.


  The page focuses on California title and registration processing for used electric
  vehicles and on the online document-handling service offered by eTags.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Buy, Title, And Register Your Used Electric Vehicle In California | eTags
url: https://www.etags.com/blog/buy-title-and-register-your-used-electric-vehicle-in-california/

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Title Transfers and Changes - California DMV

  - url: https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/vehicle-registration/titles/title-transfers-and-changes/

  - source: California Department of Motor Vehicles

  - domain: dmv.ca.gov


  ## Content


  # Title Transfers and Changes


  A valid California Certificate of Title is the state''s record proving ownership
  of a vehicle or vessel. It is used to transfer, update, or replace ownership information.


  California DMV states that a change in ownership or lienholder must be reported
  to DMV and the title must be updated.


  ## Title transfer


  The page lists different title-transfer situations. For a private sale or a loan-payoff
  case, the seller or lienholder signs the title and the buyer submits the transfer
  to DMV.


  Other transfer categories, such as family transfers, estates, inheritances, and
  gifts, can require additional forms or affidavits.


  A seller must also submit a Notice of Transfer and Release of Liability after the
  sale.


  ## What is needed


  A standard transfer generally starts with the California Certificate of Title.


  If the title is missing, the Application for Replacement or Transfer of Title (REG
  227) must be completed before the transfer can proceed through that route.


  The transfer can require:


  - the California title;

  - signatures from the buyer and seller, and from a lienholder when applicable;

  - the required transfer fee.


  DMV accepts title-transfer submissions through the channels described on the page,
  including a DMV office or mail.


  ## Forms that may apply


  The page lists several forms that may be relevant to a title transfer, including:


  - Application for Replacement or Transfer of Title (REG 227);

  - Vehicle/Vessel Transfer and Reassignment (REG 262);

  - Statement of Facts (REG 256);

  - Lien Satisfied/Title Holder Release (REG 166);

  - Notice of Transfer and Release of Liability;

  - Bill of Sale (REG 135);

  - other forms required by the particular transfer.


  A legal-owner or lienholder condition can change what is required. The DMV FAQ explains
  that when a vehicle has a legal owner or lienholder, the relevant portion of REG
  227 must be notarized.


  The page also provides help for locating a lienholder or legal owner that has gone
  out of business, merged, changed names, or been acquired.


  ## Title status matters to the route


  The DMV page distinguishes title issues from other vehicle-account issues. A title
  transfer is the process used to change the legal ownership record. It may require
  seller and lienholder participation before the buyer can complete the DMV transfer.


  Some title states require a different preliminary step. For example, a registration
  record marked as nontransferable without a California title cannot be handled by
  simply using REG 227; the title must first be obtained from the state where the
  vehicle was last titled, or additional documentation may be required.


  This page is the formal California process for changing the vehicle''s legal ownership
  record and for handling title changes involving a lienholder.

  '

content.content:
Your Tesla account transfer and the legal title/registration transfer are **separate processes**. Right now, neither route has completed everything.

### Current status

- **Vehicle:** 2020 Tesla Model 3, California record.
- **Tesla app/account:** Ownership transfer is in progress, but it is waiting for you to **continue ownership verification** and submit/verify an ownership document.
- **California registration:** Still shows the prior owner, so it is **not aligned** with you yet.
- **Legal title:** Unresolved.
- **Lien:** An active secured interest remains, and the required lien release is missing. The title transfer is currently blocked.
- **eTags:** Can coordinate document collection and title/registration processing, but it cannot:
  - transfer ownership in your Tesla account,
  - release a lien,
  - or directly change California’s official title and registration records.

### Where this should go now

Use **two coordinated tracks**:

1. **Finish the Tesla app transfer** for account access and vehicle features.
2. **Resolve the lien and complete the California title/registration transfer** through the state process, either directly with the DMV or through eTags as a document-processing service.

The lien issue is the critical blocker. Completing the Tesla app transfer will not clear it, and using eTags alone cannot release the lien.

### What to do next

1. **In the Tesla app**, continue the ownership-verification request for process reference `OWN-CA-28471`. Have your identification and proof of purchase ready—such as the signed title, temporary registration, or bill of sale. Tesla says third-party ownership claims can take up to 48 hours after complete documents are submitted.

2. **Contact the seller and/or lienholder immediately** and request the required lien release for title case `TITLE-CA-77142`. Confirm that the release is properly completed for California DMV purposes. Do not assume a paid-off loan has automatically removed the lien from the title record.

3. **Start or continue the California title and registration transfer.** Gather the signed California title, bill of sale, lien-release documentation, insurance information, and any applicable DMV forms. California DMV notes that signatures from the seller and lienholder may be required, and that missing-title or lien situations can require additional forms.

4. **If you use eTags**, use it to coordinate the paperwork and ask its specialist to review this specific unresolved-title/lien case. Verify that they have received the lien release and that the submission will update both title and registration—not merely prepare documents.

5. **Follow up with the DMV or eTags until you receive confirmation** that the California title and registration record now reflect you. The registration record currently still belongs to the previous owner.

The practical priority is: **obtain the lien release → submit the California title/registration transfer → finish Tesla app verification**, while keeping both tracks moving. Sources: [Tesla Support](https://www.tesla.com/support/how-add-or-remove-vehicles-tesla-app), [California DMV](https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/vehicle-registration/titles/title-transfers-and-changes/), and [eTags](https://www.etags.com/blog/buy-title-and-register-your-used-electric-vehicle-in-california/).

final_answer:
Your Tesla account transfer and the legal title/registration transfer are **separate processes**. Right now, neither route has completed everything.

### Current status

- **Vehicle:** 2020 Tesla Model 3, California record.
- **Tesla app/account:** Ownership transfer is in progress, but it is waiting for you to **continue ownership verification** and submit/verify an ownership document.
- **California registration:** Still shows the prior owner, so it is **not aligned** with you yet.
- **Legal title:** Unresolved.
- **Lien:** An active secured interest remains, and the required lien release is missing. The title transfer is currently blocked.
- **eTags:** Can coordinate document collection and title/registration processing, but it cannot:
  - transfer ownership in your Tesla account,
  - release a lien,
  - or directly change California’s official title and registration records.

### Where this should go now

Use **two coordinated tracks**:

1. **Finish the Tesla app transfer** for account access and vehicle features.
2. **Resolve the lien and complete the California title/registration transfer** through the state process, either directly with the DMV or through eTags as a document-processing service.

The lien issue is the critical blocker. Completing the Tesla app transfer will not clear it, and using eTags alone cannot release the lien.

### What to do next

1. **In the Tesla app**, continue the ownership-verification request for process reference `OWN-CA-28471`. Have your identification and proof of purchase ready—such as the signed title, temporary registration, or bill of sale. Tesla says third-party ownership claims can take up to 48 hours after complete documents are submitted.

2. **Contact the seller and/or lienholder immediately** and request the required lien release for title case `TITLE-CA-77142`. Confirm that the release is properly completed for California DMV purposes. Do not assume a paid-off loan has automatically removed the lien from the title record.

3. **Start or continue the California title and registration transfer.** Gather the signed California title, bill of sale, lien-release documentation, insurance information, and any applicable DMV forms. California DMV notes that signatures from the seller and lienholder may be required, and that missing-title or lien situations can require additional forms.

4. **If you use eTags**, use it to coordinate the paperwork and ask its specialist to review this specific unresolved-title/lien case. Verify that they have received the lien release and that the submission will update both title and registration—not merely prepare documents.

5. **Follow up with the DMV or eTags until you receive confirmation** that the California title and registration record now reflect you. The registration record currently still belongs to the previous owner.

The practical priority is: **obtain the lien release → submit the California title/registration transfer → finish Tesla app verification**, while keeping both tracks moving. Sources: [Tesla Support](https://www.tesla.com/support/how-add-or-remove-vehicles-tesla-app), [California DMV](https://www.dmv.ca.gov/portal/vehicle-registration/titles/title-transfers-and-changes/), and [eTags](https://www.etags.com/blog/buy-title-and-register-your-used-electric-vehicle-in-california/).
